reasoning_model_1: Model 1 accurately synthesizes the text 'This proposal, good, we have approved it.' without any errors or hallucinations. Regarding the specific instruction, the model inserts a very clear and natural pause immediately after the word 'good' (occurring roughly between 00:02 and 00:03). The prosody handles the parenthetical 'good' effectively, separating it from the rest of the sentence as requested. The audio is fluent and natural.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 fails the content accuracy criterion significantly. It hallucinates or stutters at the beginning, producing something like 'Ah his this proposal...' instead of starting cleanly with 'This proposal'. Furthermore, regarding the feature control, the audio is rushed, and there is no discernible or 'clear' pause after the word 'good'. The phrase 'good we have approved it' runs together without the requested temporal separation. The overall quality is poor due to the content error.
model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner. It followed the content script perfectly and executed the paralinguistic instruction (a clear pause after 'good') flawlessly. Model 2 failed on Content Accuracy by including hallucinations/stuttering at the start ('Ah his...') and failed to implement the requested pause, resulting in a rushed and incorrect delivery.
